stanzas:
vazhinum saavinum,
varum dhinam poi
veelinum,
nin kalal viduvenallaen.

ennullam kovil
angae undu dheivam.
adhai indha geetham
allavaa,
sollavaa,
unnai thodum
unmai allavaa.

nee vandhadhengo
naanum
vandhadhengo,
nammai ingu
onraai serthadhaen.
isaiye,
endhan munbu
unnai vaithadhaen.

pirandhadhu sittrooil
vazhvadhu olai kudilil.
irukkum ivai vandhu
ennai enna seiiyum.

megamattra vaan pola
thelindha thanneerpola
oottredukkum
isai amudham
endhan meedhu odum

nee virumbum neram
unakidhu vendum
un gavanam yaavum
pozhudhu poga theerum.

ennullam kovil
angae undu dheivam
adhai indha geetham
allavaa,
sollavaa,
unnai thodum
unmai allavaa.

oorgal koodum
thirunaalai.
thodangi vaikkum
en koottam.
mudindhaal oororam
odhungi vazha vendum.

isai dheivam
kalaivaani,
enakkarul un podhum.
oor dheivam pesaadhu
saatchi pola paarkkum.

niraindha endhan nenjam
thirandhirukkum vaanam.
kuraigal thannai thalli
unmai kondu vazhum.
enakkendru edhu undu
ingu indha mannil

ennullam kovil
angae undu dheivam.
adhai indha geetham
allavaa,
sollavaa,
unnai thodum
unmai allavaa.

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Idarinum?

The song expresses deep feelings of longing and devotion, reflecting the emotional struggles of love.

How does Idarinum fit into the plot of Thaarai Thappattai?

This song captures the protagonist's emotional turmoil and connection to love, enhancing the film's narrative.

Who is the singer and music director of Idarinum?

The song is sung by Sharreth and composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja.

When was Idarinum released?

The song was released on December 28, 2015.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Idarinum, the poignant lyrics penned by Ilaiyaraaja evoke a sense of deep yearning and emotional complexity. The song beautifully encapsulates the struggles of love and the pain of separation, making it a perfect fit for moments of reflection. As Sharreth's soulful voice carries the melody, listeners are drawn into a world of nostalgia and longing. This track not only enhances the narrative of Thaarai Thappattai but also resonates with anyone who has faced the trials of love.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Idarinum is a hauntingly beautiful melody that captures the essence of longing and love, brought to life by the soulful voice of Sharreth. With the legendary Ilaiyaraaja's masterful composition, this song resonates deeply with anyone who has experienced the bittersweet nature of relationships. Dive into the emotional depths of Thaarai Thappattai and let the music take you on a journey of heartfelt emotions.
stanzaMeanings: I will worship your feet with my heart and soul.
In life and death, every day, even if I lie, I will not let go of your feet.
When the earth shakes, I will feel it and become a pure soul.
There is a temple in my heart, that is God. Isn't this song about that, or is it about the truth that touches you?
You came, I came, we came together as one. Music, I have kept you before me.
Born in the sky, living in the nest. These things come and do something to me.
Like the rain that falls from the sky, the music nectar flows over me.
The time you desire must come, your attention will always be there until it ends.
Even if I sing sweetly, it will become a bitter medicine. Living with me will become a song for me.
There is a temple in my heart, that is God. Isn't this song about that, or is it about the truth that touches you?
On the day when the people gather, my group will start. When it ends, the people must live on.
Music is divine, the artist is my support. The village God does not speak, but watches like a witness.
My heart is full, the sky is open. The troubles push away and I live with the truth. What do I have here in this world?
There is no one to oppose, what else do I need?
There is a temple in my heart, that is God. Isn't this song about that, or is it about the truth that touches you?
